hero.title: Industries Served
hero.subtitle: Standardizing operations across healthcare, retail, logistics, and beyond for a connected world.

page.meta_description: Discover how GS1 standards drive efficiency, safety, and visibility across healthcare, retail, logistics, and more.
page.transforming_subtitle: GS1 standards are the common language of business, enabling seamless communication and interoperability worldwide.
page.why_trust_subtitle: Our neutral, non-profit platform ensures that data can be shared seamlessly between trading partners, regardless of size or sector.

healthcare-pharma.title: Healthcare & Pharma
healthcare-pharma.description: Ensure patient safety with end-to-end traceability for pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and hospital supplies using GS1 standards.
healthcare-pharma.description1: The healthcare industry faces unique challenges in ensuring patient safety, reducing medication errors, and securing the supply chain against counterfeit drugs. GS1 standards provide the foundation for end-to-end traceability, enabling manufacturers, distributors, and healthcare providers to track pharmaceuticals and medical devices from production to the point of care. By implementing Global Trade Item Numbers (GTINs) and 2D barcodes, stakeholders can instantly verify product authenticity and expiration dates.
healthcare-pharma.description2: Beyond patient safety, GS1 standards drive immense operational efficiency. Automated data capture reduces manual entry errors and streamlines inventory management in hospitals and pharmacies. With precise identification of implementation locations (GLNs) and products, healthcare systems can optimize procurement, reduce waste, and ensure that the right patient receives the right treatment at the right time. Our global standards are harmonized with regulatory requirements worldwide, making compliance seamless.

retail-fmcg.title: Retail & FMCG
retail-fmcg.description: Streamline point-of-sale, inventory management, and omnichannel commerce with globally recognized barcodes and data standards.
retail-fmcg.description1: In the fast-paced world of Retail and FMCG, consumer expectations for product availability and information are higher than ever. GS1 standards enable a smarter supply chain by providing a common language for identifying, capturing, and sharing data about products and locations. From the warehouse to the checkout counter, GTINs and barcodes ensure that products are accurately identified, reducing errors and speeding up transactions. This foundation supports critical operations like automated ordering, inventory replenishment, and seamless omnichannel experiences.
retail-fmcg.description2: Transitioning to the next generation of barcodes, such as 2D codes with GS1 Digital Link, unlocks a new dimension of consumer engagement and traceability. Retailers and brands can offer detailed product information—ingredients, sustainability credentials, and usage instructions—through a simple scan. This transparency builds trust and loyalty while simultaneously enabling advanced supply chain capabilities like dynamic pricing and efficient recall management, ensuring safer and more responsive retail operations.

logistics-supply-chain.title: Logistics & Supply Chain
logistics-supply-chain.description: Gain real-time visibility across your entire supply chain with standardized identification and EPCIS event tracking.
logistics-supply-chain.description1: Efficient logistics rely on knowing exactly where shipments are at any given moment. GS1 standards like the Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC) and Global Location Number (GLN) provide the backbone for real-time visibility across complex, multi-modal supply chains. By uniquely identifying logistic units—pallets, cases, and containers—companies can automate tracking from dispatch to delivery, significantly reducing manual interventions and the risk of lost inventory.
logistics-supply-chain.description2: Adopting standard event data sharing through EPCIS (Electronic Product Code Information Services) allows trading partners to synchronize their views of the supply chain. This interoperability fosters collaboration, enabling faster handovers, accurate demand forecasting, and optimized transport routes. Whether moving goods by land, sea, or air, GS1 standards ensure that information flows as smoothly as the physical goods, driving down costs and enhancing service levels for customers worldwide.

food-safety-agriculture.title: Food Safety & Agriculture
food-safety-agriculture.description: Meet regulatory compliance and build consumer trust with farm-to-fork traceability powered by GS1 Digital Link.
food-safety-agriculture.description1: Food safety is paramount, and consumers increasingly demand to know the story behind what they eat. GS1 standards facilitate complete farm-to-fork traceability, allowing producers, manufacturers, and retailers to track food products with precision. By capturing critical data points such as batch numbers and expiration dates in standardized barcodes, the industry can rapidly respond to food safety incidents, executing targeted recalls that minimize waste and protect public health.
food-safety-agriculture.description2: Beyond safety, standardization drives efficiency and sustainability in agriculture. Growers and packers can use Global Location Numbers (GLNs) to identify fields and packing houses, linking produce directly to its source. This granular data helps in complying with stringent regulations like FSMA 204. Furthermore, GS1 standards support fresher inventories and reduced food waste by enabling "First Expired, First Out" (FEFO) stock management, ensuring that produce reaches consumers at peak quality.

e-commerce-digital.title: E-Commerce & Digital
e-commerce-digital.description: Enable seamless product discovery, authentication, and enriched digital experiences with GS1-powered QR codes.
e-commerce-digital.description1: In the digital marketplace, accurate product data is critical for searchability and sales. GS1 GTINs are the trusted currency of e-commerce, required by major marketplaces like Amazon and Google Shopping to uniquely identify products. By using these global identifiers, brands ensure their products are discoverable, reducing listing errors and preventing conflicts with duplicate items. This foundation allows for a seamless shopping experience across platforms.
e-commerce-digital.description2: GS1 standards are bridging the gap between physical products and digital experiences. With GS1 Digital Link, a single QR code on packaging can direct consumers to a brand-owned product page, authentication services, or loyalty programs. This direct connection empowers brands to control their narrative, combat counterfeiting, and provide rich, structured data that enhances SEO. Standards-based identification ensures that the digital twin of a product is as reliable and recognizable as the physical item on the shelf.

construction-assets.title: Construction & Assets
construction-assets.description: Track fixed assets, building materials, and equipment with reliable identification standards that reduce loss and improve audits.
construction-assets.description1: The construction industry faces challenges with asset visibility, material tracking, and project delays. GS1 standards offer a robust solution by uniquely identifying tools, equipment, and building materials using Global Asset Identifiers (GIAIs) and GTINs. This enables precise tracking from the manufacturer to the job site, reducing loss and theft while ensuring that the right materials are available when needed. Digital inventories become accurate, streamlining project management and reducing downtime.
construction-assets.description2: Adopting these standards facilitates the creation of a 'digital twin' for buildings and infrastructure. By embedding standardized data into Building Information Modeling (BIM) software, stakeholders can access verified product information throughout the asset's lifecycle—from design and construction to maintenance and demolition. This traceability improves safety audits, maintenance scheduling, and regulatory compliance, ensuring that built environments are safer, more sustainable, and cost-effective to manage.

apparel-fashion.title: Apparel & Fashion
apparel-fashion.description: Optimize inventory accuracy and combat counterfeiting with item-level tagging and RFID standards tailored for the fashion industry.
apparel-fashion.description1: For the fashion industry, inventory accuracy and speed are key to profitability. GS1 standards, particularly when combined with RFID technology, revolutionize how apparel is tracked. Item-level tagging with Electronic Product Codes (EPC) allows retailers to achieve over 99% inventory accuracy, enabling rapid cycle counts and ensuring that shelves are replenished instantly. This visibility creates a seamless omnichannel experience, where customers can confidently check store availability online.
apparel-fashion.description2: Counterfeiting is a major threat to brand integrity in fashion. GS1 standards provide a secure way to authenticate products through unique serialization. By assigning a unique digital identity to every single item, brands can protect their intellectual property and reassure customers of authenticity. Furthermore, these standards support circular economy initiatives by enabling tracking for resale, repair, and recycling, helping the fashion industry move towards a more sustainable future.

government-public-sector.title: Government & Public Sector
government-public-sector.description: Enhance public procurement, safety, and operational efficiency with standardized identification and data exchange protocols.
government-public-sector.description1: Governments rely on accurate data to ensure public safety, manage resources, and enforce regulations. GS1 standards provide a proven framework for enhancing public procurement and supply chain security. By mandating standardized identification for goods and services, public agencies can improve transparency, reduce fraud, and streamline logistics for everything from office supplies to defense equipment. This interoperability facilitates better cross-border trade management and customs processes.
government-public-sector.description2: In sectors like healthcare and defense, the stakes for traceability are high. GS1 standards enable governments to track critical assets and sensitive materials with precision. This capability is essential for effective emergency response, allowing for the rapid mobilization of resources during crises. Additionally, standardized data supports better policy-making through reliable analytics, helping public sector leaders drive efficiency, reduce waste, and deliver better services to citizens.

technical-industries.title: Technical Industries
technical-industries.description: Standardizing complex technical components and machinery for better interoperability and maintenance.
technical-industries.description1: Technical industries, including aerospace, automotive, and engineering, deal with complex machinery and millions of parts. GS1 standards facilitate the direct part marking (DPM) and unique identification of components, ensuring traceability throughout their lifecycle. This is crucial for verifying the authenticity of spare parts, preventing the use of counterfeit components that could compromise safety and performance in critical systems.
technical-industries.description2: Maintenance, Repair, and Operations (MRO) are significantly optimized through standardized data. Technicians can scan a part to instantly access its maintenance history, specifications, and installation guides. This reduces downtime and improves asset utilization. Furthermore, standardized data exchange between manufacturers, suppliers, and operators ensures that digital engineering records match the physical reality, streamlining collaboration and innovation in highly technical ecosystems.

humanitarian-logistics.title: Humanitarian Logistics
humanitarian-logistics.description: Ensuring aid reaches those in need with efficient tracking and transparency in humanitarian operations.
humanitarian-logistics.description1: In humanitarian crises, speed and accuracy save lives. GS1 standards bring supply chain discipline to relief operations, ensuring that aid reaches the right people at the right time. By uniquely identifying relief items and shipments, organizations can track the flow of food, medicine, and shelter materials from donors to beneficiaries. This visibility prevents bottlenecks, reduces theft and diversion, and ensures accountability to donors and the public.
humanitarian-logistics.description2: Interoperability is a key challenge when multiple NGOs and agencies respond to a disaster. GS1 standards provide a common language for logistics, allowing different organizations to coordinate effectively. Standardized labeling and data sharing mean that a pallet packed by one agency can be seamlessly received, stored, and distributed by another. This collaboration maximizes the impact of limited resources and improving the overall effectiveness of humanitarian response efforts globally.
